context.description: Reflective philosophical prose on self-observation, shame, and the limits of expanding awareness. It uses Ouspensky and an analogy of weightlifting and a rubber band to explain why consciousness tends to return to a comfortable baseline.
normalization_notes: Normalized category to book_body_content and subcategory to theoretical_discussion based on continuous reflective prose.

rows.paragraph_text: A reflective passage explains self-observation through Ouspensky's example of noticing oneself speak automatically and feeling shame at one's own nonsense. It argues that shame arises when inner contradictions become unavoidable, so awareness is usually kept at a comfortable level to protect functioning. The passage uses weightlifting and a rubber band as analogies for the effort required to voluntarily expand awareness.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page on self-observation, shame, inner contradictions, and the difficulty of expanding awareness; references Ouspensky and uses weightlifting and rubber band analogies.
